---
title: "BCB546 R Assignment"
author: "Prince Ameyaw"
date: "2026-03-16"
output:
  pdf_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# --- Data Acquisition ---
# Note: This script uses relative paths. 
# Ensure the .txt files are in the same directory as this Rmd file.
```

```{r}
## Load library
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
# Load data 

## load fang_et_al_genotypes.txt file
genoty_data <- read_tsv("fang_et_al_genotypes.txt")
file.size("fang_et_al_genotypes.txt")

## load snp_position.txt file 
snp_pos_data <- read_tsv("snp_position.txt")
file.size("snp_position.txt")
```

```{r}
# data inspection; Verify data integrity, dimensions, and structure before processing

## fang_et_al_genotypes.txt
str(genoty_data)
dim(genoty_data)
head(genoty_data)
tail(genoty_data)

## snp_position.txt
str(snp_pos_data)
dim(snp_pos_data)
head(snp_pos_data)
tail(snp_pos_data)
```

```{r}
# data processing 

## inspecting the genotype data group i will be using to subset the data into maize and teosinte

### i used `unique` to Identify available groups to ensure correct filtering
unique(genoty_data$Group) 

## selecting maize data
maize_genotypes <- genoty_data %>% filter(Group == c("ZMMIL", "ZMMLR", "ZMMMR" ))
head(maize_genotypes)
dim(maize_genotypes)
unique(maize_genotypes$Group)

## selecting teosinte data
teosinte_genotypes <- genoty_data %>% filter(Group == c("ZMPBA", "ZMPIL", "ZMPJA"))
head(teosinte_genotypes) 
dim(teosinte_genotypes)
unique(teosinte_genotypes$Group)
```

```{r}
# transposing genotype files
## I transposed the data so that SNP_IDs become rows, allowing to join with the position map.

### maize genotypes
maize_genotypes_transposed <- t(maize_genotypes)
dim(maize_genotypes_transposed)
dim(maize_genotypes)
maize_genotypes_transposed <- as.data.frame(maize_genotypes_transposed)
colnames(maize_genotypes_transposed) <- maize_genotypes_transposed[1, ] # Set header to Sample IDs
maize_genotypes_transposed <- maize_genotypes_transposed[-1, ] # Remove the extra header row
maize_genotypes_transposed$SNP_ID <- rownames(maize_genotypes_transposed)
rownames(maize_genotypes_transposed) <- NULL
head(maize_genotypes_transposed)


### teosinte genotypes Same logic as maize above
teosinte_genotypes_transposed <- t(teosinte_genotypes)
teosinte_genotypes_transposed <- as.data.frame(teosinte_genotypes_transposed)
colnames(teosinte_genotypes_transposed) <- teosinte_genotypes_transposed[1, ]
teosinte_genotypes_transposed <- teosinte_genotypes_transposed[-1, ]
teosinte_genotypes_transposed$SNP_ID <- rownames(teosinte_genotypes_transposed)
rownames(teosinte_genotypes_transposed) <- NULL
```

```{r}
# Merging 

## Select only the essential mapping columns (ID, Chr, Pos)
snp_pos_data_needed_columns <- snp_pos_data %>% select(SNP_ID, Chromosome, Position)
```

```{r}
# joining genotype data and snp position data 

## maize genotypes and snp position (i used `dim` to qc the files to make sure they are properly joined)
merged_maize_geno_snp <- inner_join(snp_pos_data_needed_columns, maize_genotypes_transposed, by = "SNP_ID")
dim(merged_maize_geno_snp)
dim(snp_pos_data_needed_columns)
dim(maize_genotypes_transposed)

## teosinte genotypes and snp position
merged_teosinte_geno_snp <- inner_join(snp_pos_data_needed_columns, teosinte_genotypes_transposed, by = "SNP_ID")
dim(merged_teosinte_geno_snp)
dim(snp_pos_data_needed_columns)
dim(teosinte_genotypes_transposed)
```

```{r}
# maize: 10 files (1 for each chromosome) with SNPs ordered based on increasing position values and with missing data encoded by this symbol: ?

## Filter for chromosomes 1 through 10
maize_subset <- merged_maize_geno_snp[merged_maize_geno_snp$Chromosome %in% 1:10, ]

# Split the data frame into a LIST of 10 data frames (one per chromosome)
maize_list <- split(maize_subset, maize_subset$Chromosome)

# Define the  function, sort and define filename for saving
process_and_save_per_chr_increase <- function(chr) {
  df <- maize_list[[chr]]
  df_sorted <- df[order(as.numeric(df$Position)), ]
  file_name <- paste0("maize_chr", chr, "_increasing.txt")
  write.table(df_sorted, file = `file_name`, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
}
# use Apply to run through all chromosome lists
lapply(names(maize_list), process_and_save_per_chr_increase)
```

```{r}
# maize: 10 files (1 for each chromosome) with SNPs ordered based on decreasing position values and with missing data encoded by this symbol: -

process_and_save_per_chr_decrease <- function(chr) {
  df <- maize_list[[chr]]
   # Replace "??" with "-" across the entire data frame
  df[df == "?/?"] <- "-/-"
  df_sorted <- df[order(as.numeric(df$Position), decreasing = TRUE), ]
  file_name <- paste0("maize_chr", chr, "_decreasing.txt")
  write.table(df_sorted, file = `file_name`, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
}
# use Apply to run through all chromosome lists
lapply(names(maize_list), process_and_save_per_chr_decrease)
```


```{r}
# teosinte (same logic as above)
# teosinte: 10 files (1 for each chromosome) with SNPs ordered based on increasing position values and with missing data encoded by this symbol: ?

## Filter for chromosomes 1 through 10
teosinte_subset <- merged_teosinte_geno_snp[merged_teosinte_geno_snp$Chromosome %in% 1:10, ]

# Split the data frame into a LIST of 10 data frames (one per chromosome)
teosinte_list <- split(teosinte_subset, teosinte_subset$Chromosome)
process_and_save_per_chr_increase <- function(chr) {
  df <- teosinte_list[[chr]]
  df_sorted <- df[order(as.numeric(df$Position)), ]
  file_name <- paste0("teosinte_chr", chr, "_increasing.txt")
  write.table(df_sorted, file = `file_name`, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
}
# use Apply to run through all chromosome lists
lapply(names(teosinte_list), process_and_save_per_chr_increase)
```

```{r}
# teosinte files (1 for each chromosome) with SNPs ordered based on decreasing position values and with missing data encoded by this symbol: -

process_and_save_per_chr_decrease <- function(chr) {
  df <- teosinte_list[[chr]]
   # Replace "??" with "-" across the entire data frame
  df[df == "?/?"] <- "-/-"
  df_sorted <- df[order(as.numeric(df$Position), decreasing = TRUE), ]
  file_name <- paste0("teosinte_chr", chr, "_decreasing.txt")
  write.table(df_sorted, file = `file_name`, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
}
# use Apply to run through all chromosome lists
lapply(names(teosinte_list), process_and_save_per_chr_decrease)
```


```{r}
# Visuaization

## Prepare unified dataset for plotting
maize_data <- merged_maize_geno_snp %>% mutate(Group = "Maize")
teosinte_data <- merged_teosinte_geno_snp %>% mutate(Group = "Teosinte")

### i mae these column the same type of variable to facilitate with combining the dataset
maize_data <- maize_data %>%
  mutate(across(c(SNP_ID, Chromosome, Position), as.character), Group = "Maize")

teosinte_data <- teosinte_data %>%
  mutate(across(c(SNP_ID, Chromosome, Position), as.character), Group = "Teosinte")

# Combine datasets
combined_data <- bind_rows(maize_data, teosinte_data)

# Remove rows with unknown chromosome/position
combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(Chromosome) & Chromosome != "unknown") %>%
  mutate(Chromosome = factor(Chromosome, levels = c(1:10)))

# Visualization 1: SNPs per Chromosome 

## Question: What is the distribution of SNPs on and across chromosomes? ##Are there more SNP positions in maize or teosinte individuals?

ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Chromosome, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of SNPs across Chromosomes",
       x = "Chromosome Number",
       y = "Number of SNP Positions",
       fill = "Group") +
  theme_classic()

ggsave("snp_distribution.png", width = 7, height = 5)

# Visualization 2: Missing Data and Heterozygosity 
# Question: What is the proportion of homozygous, heterozygous, and missing data?

# Reshape data to long format for analysis
# I exclude the metadata columns to focus on sample genotypes
genotype_data <- combined_data %>%
  select( -SNP_ID, -Chromosome, -Position) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Group, names_to = "Sample", values_to = "Genotype")

# Define genotype categories
genotype_summary <- genotype_data %>%
  mutate(Status = case_when(
    Genotype == "?/?" ~ "Missing",
    substr(Genotype, 1, 1) == substr(Genotype, 3, 3) ~ "Homozygous",
    TRUE ~ "Heterozygous"
  ))

# Plotting the proportions normalized to 100%
ggplot(genotype_summary, aes(x = Group, fill = Status)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Proportion of Genotype Status by Group",
       x = "Group",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "Status") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_classic()
ggsave("genotype_proportions.png", width = 6, height = 5)

# visualization 3 
## Plot by individual samples (top 50 for readability)
sample_subset <- genotype_summary %>%
  filter(Sample %in% unique(genotype_summary$Sample)[1:50])

ggplot(sample_subset, aes(x = Sample, fill = Status)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Genotype Status for Selected Samples",
       x = "Sample ID",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "Status") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_classic()
ggsave("Genotype_Status_Selected_Samples.png", width = 10, height = 6)

# visualization 4
## Detailed view of Homozygous, Heterozygous, and Missing data per chromosome, side-by-side for Maize and Teosinte.
combined_long <- combined_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(SNP_ID, Chromosome, Position, Group), 
               names_to = "Sample", 
               values_to = "Genotype") %>%
  mutate(Status = case_when(
    Genotype == "?/?" ~ "Missing",
    substr(Genotype, 1, 1) == substr(Genotype, 3, 3) ~ "Homozygous",
    TRUE ~ "Heterozygous"
  ))

#  Plot using the new 'combined_long' object
ggplot(combined_long, aes(x = factor(Chromosome), fill = Status)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  facet_grid(~Group) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Homozygous" = "#377eb8", 
                               "Heterozygous" = "#ff7f00", 
                               "Missing" = "grey80")) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title = "Homozygous vs Heterozygous SNPs per Chromosome",
       x = "Chromosome",
       y = "Proportion",
       fill = "Genotype Status")
ggsave("genotype_per_chromosome.png", width = 10, height = 6)
```
